explanation: This property ensures that at any given time, it is always possible for the user to eventually *initiate the action of enabling* the leak compensation feature. This captures the 'enable' aspect of the requirement and the 'at any time' aspect, implying that the user can always attempt to activate it.

This property ensures that at any given time, it is always possible for the user to eventually *initiate the action of disabling* the leak compensation feature. This captures the 'disable' aspect of the requirement and the 'at any time' aspect, implying that the user can always attempt to deactivate it.

This property ensures that at any given time, if the leak compensation feature is currently disabled (represented by `!leakCompensationActive`), it is always possible for the user to eventually enable it (represented by `eventually satisfy enableLeakCompensation`). This captures one half of the requirement for the user to be able to toggle the feature's state freely and at any moment.
decision2_substring: The user shall be able to disable/enable the leak compensation feature

explanation: This property ensures that at any given time, if the leak compensation feature is currently enabled (represented by `leakCompensationActive`), it is always possible for the user to eventually disable it (represented by `eventually satisfy !enableLeakCompensation`). This captures the other half of the requirement for the user to be able to toggle the feature's state freely and at any moment.
decision2_substring: The user shall be able to disable/enable the leak compensation feature

explanation: This property ensures that whenever the user attempts to enable the feature (`enableLeakCompensation`), it must immediately become active (`leakCompensationActive`). This reflects direct and immediate control for enabling.
decision1_substring: The user shall be able to disable/enable the leak compensation feature at any time.

explanation: This property states that whenever the user attempts to disable the feature (`!enableLeakCompensation`), it must immediately become inactive (`!leakCompensationActive`). This reflects direct and immediate control for disabling.
decision1_substring: The user shall be able to disable/enable the leak compensation feature at any time.

explanation: This property states that whenever the user attempts to disable the feature (`!enableLeakCompensation`), it must immediately become inactive (`!leakCompensationActive`). This reflects direct and immediate control for disabling.
decision1_substring: The user shall be able to disable/enable the leak compensation feature at any time.
